pages.full_text:
Alto Aragón

old town is up on the hill. Charles swam. [?Ainsa] *** (J+C?) **** (S+E) is across the river from the town. There is a long campsite / lovely pool, lots of showers (warm
pages.notes: Page is dominated by 8 photographs (4 rows x 2 columns) showing old stone buildings, doorways, and architectural details. The caption 'Alto Aragón' is written vertically alongside the photos. The handwritten text at the bottom of the page is a continuation of diary narrative, cut off at the right by the page edge / gutter. The place name after 'Charles swam.' is partially legible — could read 'Ainsa' (a town in Alto Aragón, Huesca, Spain) or possibly 'Arnsa'; the transcribed text shows a star-rating notation which may refer to swimming spots or campsites for individuals. Text sentence is incomplete, continuing on the right page.

pages.full_text:
+ very- well-designed shower cubicles. Eve washed clothes + J, C+E hung them on the line at 8.30.pm. I had spent the 30min while the machine washed chopping the salad. After hanging the clothes we had supper. ($3.80 token from camp supermarket).
		During the night I was woked by a flash, then lots of flashes + rumbles. Charles + Eve dashed out to get in the clothes (nearly dry). Simon saw to the tent + John wondered what was going on.
		__Friday August 8th__
		We've had a lazy morning, playing bridge + swimming. During lunch + during the afternoon nap it) rained. Tea at 4.30.
		There is a huge mix of campers - Catalan Spanish campers with children, 2 Dutch guys in a tent + Porche, German (?) bikers - very disciplined, arrived in leathers & put up 2 tents straight away in blazing heat, a biker with a tent + 4
				pedal
pages.notes: Handwritten page number '35' visible in top-right corner. Text continues from the left page (mid-sentence). The date 'Friday August 8th' is underlined. The year cannot be determined from the page itself — the entry is for a trip to Alto Aragón (northern Spain). Friday August 8th falls on: 2003, 2008, 2014, 2019 — given volume B covers 2003–2019, year assigned tentatively as null; iso_guess set to 2003-08-08 with very low confidence as a placeholder. The word 'woked' is the diary's own spelling (not corrected). The text ends mid-sentence ('a biker with a tent + 4 / pedal') suggesting it continues on the next page. '$3.80 token from camp supermarket' — the superscript word above 'token' reads 'camp'. The notation '(it)' in 'nap it) rained' may be a parenthetical aside or transcription artefact. 'Porche' likely means 'Porsche'.
